description: A non-conforming value is REJECTED at the refinement-type boundary at runtime (§53.3.3 / §53.9) — the enforcement that makes a refinement type 'stronger than a state validator'. process(@amount * 3) = process(15000) passes a value that VIOLATES number(>0 && <10000) (>= 10000). The compiler-generated boundary check throws E-CONTRACT-001-RT BEFORE the assignment is applied, so @amount RETAINS its prior value (5000). Observed via the retained cell value + DOM. (E-CONTRACT-001-RT is a runtime error, not a compile code, so it does not appear in the compile `codes` set — the RT observable is the retained-prior-value.)
rationale: §53.3.3 if the predicate evaluates false at runtime the boundary check SHALL throw E-CONTRACT-001-RT before the assignment; the variable retains its prior value. 15000 violates <10000 -> assignment rejected, @amount stays 5000.
